response.justification: While the candidate brings exceptional geospatial domain expertise and distributed systems thinking, the lack of recent modern C++ experience is a critical blocker for a mid-senior role where C++ is the primary technology and the focus is on designing critical routing pipeline components. His last commercial C++ work was 11 years ago; his recent 4+ years have been heavily JavaScript/TypeScript-focused at full-stack roles. Ramping up on modern C++ would require significant onboarding time that mid-senior positions typically don't accommodate.
strengths: Deep geospatial and navigation domain expertise from founding GISCollective and building multiple GIS applications, directly addressing the role's routing/mapping requirements
Proven distributed systems and high-performance systems thinking from Dunnhumby/Sociomantic work handling millions of requests/second with low-latency optimization
Strong engineering discipline with emphasis on TDD, quality assurance, and learning agility demonstrated across multiple languages and domains
concerns: No modern C++ commercial experience—last C++ work was 11 years ago (2014–2015) at National Instruments; role requires current C++ proficiency as primary technology
Recent 4+ years (2021–present) heavily concentrated in JavaScript/TypeScript and full-stack web roles, diverging from systems-level programming required for routing pipeline work
Navigation systems experience is geospatial/data collection focused (GISCollective, Green Map) rather than production routing engines or tile optimization pipelines

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and performance optimization background
